perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, "Confidential Information" shall include all information or material that has or could have commercial value or other utility in the business in which Disclosing Party is engaged. If Confidential Information is in writt en form, the Disclosing Party shall label or stamp the materials with the word "Confidential" or some similar warning. If Confidential Information is transmitted orally, the Disclosing Party shall promptly provide a writing indicating that such oral commun ication constituted Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, "Confidential Data" shall include all information or material that has or could have commercial value or other utility in the business in which Disclosing Party is engaged. If Confidential Data is in writt en form, the Disclosing Party shall label or stamp the materials with the word "Confidential" or some similar warning. If Confidential Data is transmitted orally, the Disclosing Party shall promptly provide a writing indicating that such oral commun ication constituted Confidential Data.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Confidential Data" in the definition section introduces an inconsistency. The rest of the document uses "Confidential Information," so it's now ambiguous if "Confidential Data" is the same as "Confidential Information". This creates uncertainty about what the Receiving Party is actually obligated to protect.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Receiving Party shall not, without prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's own benefit, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their b enefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Inform ation immediately if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Confidential Information" to "Confidential Data" in the definition section creates a direct conflict with the "Contradicted Text", which consistently uses "Confidential Information". This misalignment introduces ambiguity, making it unclear whether the obligations related to "Confidential Information" apply to "Confidential Data", thus creating a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall safeguard the Confidential Information but not necessarily for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By stating the Receiving Party doesn't necessarily hold the information for the exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party, this directly contradicts the original intent of a Non-Disclosure Agreement and creates a conflict with the defined obligations of confidentiality and exclusive benefit.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Receiving Party shall not, without prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's own benefit, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their b enefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Inform ation immediately if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates that the Receiving Party hold Confidential Information for the 'sole and exclusive benefit' of the Disclosing Party. The changed text weakens this by stating it's 'not necessarily' for the sole and exclusive benefit. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which reiterates the 'sole and exclusive benefit' requirement and prohibits the Receiving Party from using the information for their own benefit or to the detriment of the Disclosing Party. This contradiction introduces ambiguity and undermines the core principle of confidentiality and exclusive benefit, as the Receiving Party's obligations are no longer clearly defined. Therefore, the 'Misaligned Terminology' perturbation, has created an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall allow access to Confidential Information to its affiliates and subsidiaries without the requirement of nondisclosure agreements.
perturbation.explanation: This alteration creates a significant contradiction. The original text mandates restricted access and nondisclosure agreements. The change removes the need for nondisclosure agreements with affiliates, meaning the Confidential Information could be disseminated within the Receiving Party's corporate structure without legal protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Receiving Party shall not, without prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's own benefit, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their b enefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Inform ation immediately if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates nondisclosure agreements for anyone accessing confidential information. The changed text explicitly allows affiliates and subsidiaries access without these agreements. The contradicted text reiterates the requirement for strict confidence and restricted access with nondisclosure agreements. Therefore, the changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text by creating an exception to the nondisclosure requirement, thus creating a clear and impactful contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Inform ation immediately if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall archive records related to the engagement but is not obligated to return them upon request.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the return obligation directly contradicts the core purpose of the NDA, which is to protect the Disclosing Party's confidential information and ensure its return. This change grants the Receiving Party the right to retain these records, potentially allowing them to use or disclose the information after the agreement terminates.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The nondisclosure provisions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement and Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall r emain in effect until the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or until Disclosing Party sends Receiving Party written notice releasing Receiving Party from this Agreement, whichever occurs first.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the Receiving Party to return confidential information upon request, whereas the changed text states the Receiving Party is not obligated to return records. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states the Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect until certain conditions are met, implying the eventual return or destruction of said information. The change introduces a right to retain records, conflicting with the duty to hold them in confidence indefinitely and potentially return them.

perturbation.original_text: The nondisclosure provisions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement and Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall r emain in effect until the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or until Disclosing Party sends Receiving Party written notice releasing Receiving Party from this Agreement, whichever occurs first.
perturbation.changed_text: The confidentiality obligations under this agreement shall terminate automatically after one year, regardless of whether the information remains a trade secret.
perturbation.explanation: This change significantly weakens the agreement. The original clause ensures confidentiality continues as long as the information is a trade secret. The revised clause sets a fixed one-year term, after which the Receiving Party is no longer bound by confidentiality, even if the information remains proprietary and valuable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Receiving Party shall not, without prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's own benefit, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their b enefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Inform ation immediately if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction. The original text ensures confidentiality as long as the information remains a trade secret, while the changed text limits the confidentiality obligation to one year. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which states the Receiving Party shall hold the Confidential Information in strictest confidence, implying an indefinite period, and restricts its use and disclosure. The change effectively nullifies the indefinite confidentiality requirement outlined in the contradicted text after one year, even if the information remains a trade secret, creating a strong contradiction.
